nav.title: Rotten Chamber
hero.tagline: A single-player social deduction puzzle game
hero.subtitle: Analyze statements · Cross-reference clues · Arrest the corrupt
screenshots.title: Screenshots
screenshots.subtitle: See the chamber in action
howto.title: How to Play
howto.subtitle: Uncover the corruption in four steps
howto.intro: A single-player social deduction puzzle solved through logic alone.
howto.step1.title: Read
howto.step1.desc: Each official makes a statement. Some tell the truth. Some don't. Read carefully.
howto.step2.title: Deduce
howto.step2.desc: Cross-reference statements against each other. If two claims contradict, someone is lying.
howto.step3.title: Investigate
howto.step3.desc: Use abilities and hired professionals to test your theory. Mark suspects with stamps and ribbons.
howto.step4.title: Arrest
howto.step4.desc: Find every corrupt official. Three wrong arrests and you lose. Arrest the Mayor by mistake and it's over instantly.
roles.title: The Chamber
roles.subtitle: 15 unique roles. Each with a statement, an agenda and something to hide.
roles.intro: Every role speaks. Not every role tells the truth.
roles.more: ...and 6 more innocent roles to discover in-game.
roles.corrupt_mystery_desc: Corrupt officials disguise themselves as innocent roles. Some blackmail, some silence, some convert loyalties. Every lie is a clue if you know where to look.
role.mayor.name: Mayor
role.mayor.web_desc: The only face you can trust. Sits at the head of the table and vouches for one official. But is that trust deserved?
role.legal_advocate.name: Legal Advocate
role.legal_advocate.web_desc: Points at someone and asks: are you lying? One question. One answer. Use it wisely.
role.council_chair.name: Council Chair
role.council_chair.web_desc: Knows whether the corrupt are sitting together or scattered. A single yes-or-no that changes everything.
role.police_chief.name: Police Chief
role.police_chief.web_desc: Feels the tension next door. Tells you if corruption is sitting right beside them.
role.crook.name: Crook
role.silencer.name: Silencer
role.blackmailer.name: Blackmailer
role.converter.name: Converter
role.manipulator.name: Manipulator
features.title: Features
features.subtitle: What makes Rotten Chamber unique
features.intro: A logic puzzle game built on deduction, not luck.
feature.deduction.title: Pure Deduction
feature.deduction.desc: No dice rolls. No card draws. Every logic puzzle is solvable through deduction alone. If you got it wrong, you missed something.
feature.campaign.title: Campaign Mode
feature.campaign.desc: 45 levels across three acts. New roles and corrupt abilities unlock as you advance. Lose a level and your entire run is over.
feature.custom.title: Custom Games
feature.custom.desc: Choose the chamber size, the number of corrupt officials, the difficulty and which roles appear. Build exactly the puzzle you want.
feature.professionals.title: Hired Professionals
feature.professionals.desc: Earn rare specialists between levels. Deploy them as one-shot abilities: interrogate a suspect, reveal an alignment or reset a used power.
feature.annotation.title: Annotation Tools
feature.annotation.desc: Mark cards with stamps (innocent, corrupt, suspicious) and color ribbons to track your deductions as clues build up.
feature.tutorial.title: Interactive Tutorial
feature.tutorial.desc: A guided tutorial walks you through reading statements, using abilities and making your first arrest. Learn by doing, not by reading.
narrative.p1: Werewolf, Mafia, Among Us, Town of Salem, Feign, Blood on the Clocktower. Social deduction has always been about reading people. A group sits in a circle, someone is lying and everyone argues about who.
narrative.p2: We wanted to ask a different question: what if you could strip all of that away and leave only the logic?
narrative.p3: Rotten Chamber is what happened. A solo social deduction puzzle game where every statement follows rules, every lie leaves a trace and every puzzle has exactly one solution. No dice. No chance. No other players needed.
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Common questions about this social deduction puzzle game
faq.q1.question: What is a social deduction game?
faq.q1.answer: A social deduction game is a genre where players must figure out who among them is lying or hiding their true role. Traditionally multiplayer, Rotten Chamber reimagines this as a single-player logic puzzle where you analyze statements and cross-reference clues to identify the corrupt.
faq.q2.question: Can you play social deduction games alone?
faq.q2.answer: Yes. Rotten Chamber is a solo social deduction game designed from the ground up for a single player. Instead of reading other players, you read role-based statements and use pure logic to deduce who is corrupt.
faq.q3.question: What makes Rotten Chamber different from other social deduction games?
faq.q3.answer: Most social deduction games like Werewolf, Mafia or Among Us require a group. Rotten Chamber removes the social element and replaces it with a logic puzzle: every statement follows deterministic rules, every level is solvable through deduction alone and there is no randomness in the outcome.
faq.q4.question: How does the logic puzzle gameplay work?
faq.q4.answer: Each official around a circular table makes a statement based on their role. Innocent officials tell the truth while corrupt officials lie. You cross-reference these statements, use active abilities to test your theories and arrest those you believe are corrupt. Three wrong arrests and you lose.
faq.q5.question: Is Rotten Chamber a multiplayer game?
faq.q5.answer: No. Rotten Chamber is purely single-player. The game features 45 campaign levels across three acts, custom game modes and an interactive tutorial, all designed for solo play.
faq.q6.question: Is Rotten Chamber available on Steam?
faq.q6.answer: Rotten Chamber is currently in development. The Steam page will be available soon. Add it to your wishlist to get notified when the social deduction puzzle game launches.
presskit.title: Press Kit
presskit.overview.title: Game Overview
presskit.description.title: Description
presskit.description.ultra_short: A single-player social deduction puzzle game built around logic, contradictions and high-stakes arrests.
presskit.description.short: Rotten Chamber is a single-player social deduction puzzle game. Analyze role-based statements around a council table, test your theories with targeted abilities and arrest every corrupt official before three wrong calls end the investigation.
presskit.description.store: A single-player social deduction puzzle game where you read a corrupt city council through logic alone. Cross-check statements, use investigation abilities and arrest the corrupt before three mistakes or one bad Mayor arrest ends the case.
presskit.description.medium: Rotten Chamber reimagines social deduction as a solo puzzle game. Each official at the table speaks according to their role. Clean officials tell the truth. Corrupt officials lie, disguise themselves, silence witnesses, blackmail neighbors or quietly turn allies against you. Your job is to cross-reference every statement, test key suspects with limited abilities and make the right arrests before the chamber collapses into chaos.
presskit.description.long_p1: Rotten Chamber is a single-player social deduction puzzle game set around a council table full of officials, witnesses and hidden corruption. Every seat produces information. Every claim can be checked against another. Some officials are truthful. Others are lying on purpose. The challenge is to separate solid testimony from planted deception and build a case strong enough to survive your next arrest.
presskit.description.long_p2: The game takes the language of social deduction and strips away bluffing, table talk and group play. What remains is a logic puzzle built from roles, contradictions, adjacency rules and hidden corruption effects. Corrupt officials do not only lie. They also disguise themselves as clean roles, silence crucial voices, force innocent neighbors to speak falsely and convert trusted allies into new threats.
presskit.description.long_p3: Players investigate each chamber by reading statements, using active role abilities and deploying one-shot professionals that can reveal alignment, expose lie status or reset a spent ability. The cost of a bad call is always present: three wrong arrests end the case and arresting an innocent Mayor ends it immediately.
presskit.description.long_p4: Rotten Chamber includes a 45-level campaign across three acts, custom games with adjustable table size and corrupt count, deterministic seeds and shareable level codes, an in-game annotation system for tracking deductions and English/Turkish language support. It is built for players who enjoy reasoning through information, spotting contradictions and solving cases through logic rather than luck.
presskit.description.boilerplate: Rotten Chamber is an in-development single-player social deduction puzzle game for PC. Built in Godot, it challenges players to investigate a city council through role-based statements, hidden corruption effects and high-stakes arrests. With a 45-level campaign, custom game options, deterministic case sharing and full English/Turkish support, Rotten Chamber is designed for players who want the structure of a logic puzzle with the tension of a social deduction game.
presskit.features.title: Key Features
presskit.features.f1: Social deduction rebuilt as a single-player logic puzzle
presskit.features.f2: 15 roles with distinct statement logic, including 5 corrupt roles with disruptive powers
presskit.features.f3: 45 campaign levels across 3 acts with escalating complexity and difficulty
presskit.features.f4: Active investigation abilities that verify truthfulness, compare alignments or count corruption within a group
presskit.features.f5: One-shot hired professionals that help break deadlocks without replacing deduction
presskit.features.f6: High-stakes arrest structure: three mistakes lose the case and an innocent Mayor arrest is an instant failure
presskit.features.f7: Custom games with adjustable table size, corrupt count, difficulty and role setup
presskit.features.f8: Deterministic seeds and shareable level codes for replayable and reproducible cases
presskit.features.f9: Card stamps and color ribbons for player-driven note taking and deduction tracking
presskit.features.f10: Full tutorial support and English/Turkish localization
presskit.why.title: Why It Stands Out
presskit.why.w1: Rotten Chamber delivers the feel of social deduction without requiring other players.
presskit.why.w2: Its tension comes from logic pressure and irreversible arrests, not reflexes or random combat outcomes.
presskit.why.w3: Corruption changes how information behaves: lies can spread, witnesses can be silenced and trusted roles can be compromised.
presskit.why.w4: The political chamber setting gives the game a distinct identity compared to village, party or sci-fi social deduction formats.

presskit.faq.title: Press FAQ
presskit.faq.a1: Rotten Chamber is a single-player social deduction puzzle game in which players investigate a city council by reading role-based statements, testing claims and arresting corrupt officials.
presskit.faq.q2: How is it different from traditional social deduction games?
presskit.faq.a2: Traditional social deduction games depend on bluffing, social reads and group discussion. Rotten Chamber keeps the hidden-role tension but rebuilds it as a solo logic puzzle where outcomes are driven by rules, contradictions and deduction.
presskit.faq.a3: No. Rotten Chamber is built specifically as a single-player experience.
presskit.faq.q4: What kind of progression does the game have?
presskit.faq.a4: The game includes a 45-level campaign across three acts, plus custom games that let players change table size, corruption count, difficulty and role setup.
presskit.faq.a5: Yes. Custom games, deterministic seeds and shareable level codes make it easy to replay or share exact puzzle setups.
presskit.branding.title: Brand Guidelines
presskit.branding.rule1: Always display the logo on a dark background (#080A14 or darker)
presskit.branding.rule2: Maintain clear space around the logo equal to the height of the letter "R"
presskit.branding.rule3: Do not stretch, rotate or alter the logo colors
